metadata.defaultTitle: Dulce — Your glucose on your Lock Screen, watch and family's phone
metadata.defaultDescription: Dulce shows your FreeStyle Libre glucose on your iPhone Lock Screen, Apple Watch and widgets — with alerts and family sharing. Made by a T1D, in Spanish and English.
metadata.keywords: type 1 diabetes app, glucose on Apple Watch, Live Activity glucose, LibreLinkUp app, FreeStyle Libre companion, glucose widget iPhone, CGM app Spanish, time in range, diabetes logbook

home.title: Dulce — Your glucose on your Lock Screen, watch and family's phone
home.description: See your Libre glucose everywhere: Lock Screen, Apple Watch, widgets. Alerts and family sharing built in.

hero.eyebrow: Launching soon on the App Store
hero.headline: Made by a T1D,
hero.subheadline: Your glucose on your Lock Screen, your wrist and your family's phone — live, without opening an app.
hero.trustNote: No spam. We'll let you know when it's ready. Unsubscribe anytime.
hero.screenshotAlt: Dulce app home screen showing a glucose reading of 131 mg/dL in range, a 12-hour chart and time in range, next to an Apple Watch with the Dulce complication

adult.title: If you live with type 1 diabetes
adult.body: Keep an effortless logbook, understand your trends, and let the people who care about you stay in the loop when you choose to.

parent.title: If you care for a child with T1D
parent.body: Follow their glucose in real time, get alerts when something looks off, and share with their endo — no paperwork.

doctor.title: If you're an endocrinologist
doctor.body: Your patient shares the full picture with you — trends, time in range, and time-of-day patterns.

features.title: Everything a CGM companion should do

connect.title: Connect your Libre
connect.body: Sign in with LibreLinkUp and your glucose flows in automatically, minute by minute. Your credentials never leave your device's secure keychain.

everywhere.title: On every screen
everywhere.body: Live Activity on the Lock Screen and Dynamic Island, home-screen widgets, and a native Apple Watch app. The number is just there.

alerts.title: Alerts you can trust
alerts.body: Low and high alerts with your own thresholds, quiet hours, and a louder alarm for very low glucose — including at night.

diary.title: A complete logbook
diary.body: Log carbs, insulin and glucose in seconds with a quick slider. Everything lands in one timeline you can actually read.

trends.title: Trends that make sense
trends.body: Time in range, average and GMI over 7, 14, 30 or 90 days — so you understand what's working and what isn't.

share.title: Family can follow
share.body: Invite the people you trust with a link. They see your glucose live and can send you a nudge — and you can revoke access anytime.

integrations.title: Works with what you already use
integrations.footer: Apple Health and Apple Watch are trademarks of Apple Inc. FreeStyle Libre and LibreLinkUp are trademarks of Abbott. Dexcom is a trademark of Dexcom, Inc.

madeBy.title: Because the apps weren't built for us
madeBy.body: I've had type 1 diabetes for years. I got tired of unlocking my phone, opening an app and waiting on a spinner just to see one number. So I built Dulce for myself first — then my family asked for it too. Now the number is on my lock screen and my watch, and the people I love can check I'm fine without calling me.

finalCta.title: Be first to know when Dulce lands
finalCta.subtitle: Dulce will be free to download on the App Store. Pro features come with a 14-day free trial.

showcase.title: Your number, wherever you look
showcase.subtitle: Real screenshots from Dulce on iPhone and Apple Watch. No mock-ups.

shots.widgets: Dulce widgets: Dynamic Island, Lock Screen bar, home-screen chart and number widgets, and Apple Watch complications
shots.liveActivity: Live Activity settings in Dulce — choosing how glucose appears on the Lock Screen and Dynamic Island
shots.followers: Followers screen in Dulce — inviting family to see your glucose in real time, with a nudge message saying I'm OK
shots.logbook: Dulce logbook with time in range summary and the quick entry slider logging 5.5 units of insulin
shots.watchFace: Apple Watch face with the Dulce glucose complication showing 91 mg/dL and a trend chart

showcase.watchBody: A native Apple Watch app with watch-face complications and alerts — glance at your glucose without reaching for your phone.

features.title: Features
features.description: Everything Dulce does for you, without showing any sensitive health data.

intro.headline: Focused. Well crafted.
intro.subheadline: Dulce doesn't try to replace your endo or your CGM. What it does, it does well.

connect.title: Connect your Libre in under a minute
connect.body: Sign in with your LibreLinkUp account and Dulce keeps your glucose up to date in the background — on your phone, your watch and your Lock Screen.
bullets: Dexcom and Nightscout are on the roadmap

diary.title: Your logbook, in seconds
diary.body: Logging what you eat, inject or do should be instant. That's why Dulce is designed for quick entries — no tabs, no endless forms.
bullets: Carbs, insulin (bolus and basal), exercise, notes
Quick timestamps: now, 15 min ago, 1 h ago, custom
Filterable history: today, last 7, 14 or 30 days

trends.title: Understand your patterns
trends.body: Trends matter more than any single reading. Dulce shows you the full picture across 7, 14, 30 or 90 days — including what happens in the morning, afternoon and night.
bullets: Time in range (TIR) against your personal goal

share.title: Share only what you choose
share.body: Invite your partner, your mother or your endocrinologist. They can see your glucose in real time, get alerts, or both — you decide. You can revoke access anytime.
bullets: Invite by link — no accounts to create
Followers see your glucose live and get alerts
They can send you a nudge; you reply "I'm OK" with one tap

watch.title: Your glucose on your wrist
watch.body: Dulce ships with an Apple Watch app and complications that show your latest reading without pulling out your phone. If data is stale, you'll see it clearly — never a misleading number.
bullets: Safe mode when data hasn't refreshed in over 15 min

privacy.title: Privacy by default
privacy.body: Dulce is designed to process your data on your device whenever possible. CGM credentials are stored in the system's secure keychain. We don't sell your data — full stop.
bullets: Health data stored locally by default

disclaimer.title: Important notice
disclaimer.body: Dulce **is not a medical device**. It does not diagnose, treat, cure or prevent any disease. Treatment decisions should always be made with your healthcare team.

beta.title: Dulce private beta
beta.description: Join the closed group of testers who will try Dulce before launch.

hero.headline: Be part of the private beta
hero.subheadline: We're looking for a small group of people with T1D (or carers) in Spain and Mexico to test Dulce during summer 2026. Your feedback will shape the version that ships to everyone.

what.title: What's involved?
items: Early access to the app (TestFlight for iOS)
Direct channel with the team (a small WhatsApp group)
Your name in the credits of the first release, if you want

who.title: Who are we looking for?
items: You live with type 1 diabetes or care for someone who does
You use a CGM (Libre, Dexcom or Nightscout)
You live in Spain or Mexico
You have an iPhone with iOS 17 or later

form.title: Tell us about you

options.none: I don't use a CGM yet

notes.label: Anything else you want to share (optional)
notes.placeholder: For example: how long you've had T1D, what you'd love Dulce to do…

form.consent: I have read and accept the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.
form.success: Got it. We'll be in touch soon.

about.title: About Dulce
about.description: A glucose app made by a T1D, for the Spanish-speaking community.

hero.headline: Why Dulce exists
hero.subheadline: The short version: I have type 1 diabetes, the apps I tried made my day harder, and I could write code.

story.title: The story
story.body:
My name is Giorgio. I've lived with type 1 diabetes for years, and Dulce started as an app I built for myself.

If you live with T1D, you know the routine. You check your glucose before coffee, before driving, before sleep — dozens of times a day. Every check meant unlocking my phone, opening an app, waiting for it to load. And when my partner wanted to know I was fine, she had to text me and wait.

So one evening I started building the thing I actually wanted: the number on my lock screen without opening anything. Then it spread — to my watch face, to a widget, to an alarm that wakes me when I'm dropping at night. When my family asked to see my readings on their own phones, that became the followers feature.

I built it in Spanish first, because that's the language my diabetes lives in, and most apps treat Spanish as an afterthought.

Dulce will never tell you how much insulin to take — that's between you and your doctor. It just tries to make living with the numbers lighter. If that's what you've been missing too, I hope it helps.

privacy.title: Your data, yours
privacy.body: We don't sell data. We process what we can on your device. We comply with GDPR by conviction, not just obligation.

community.title: Community first
community.body: Dulce gets better when the T1D community has its say. Feedback goes straight to me — there's no support department in between.

honesty.title: Medical honesty
honesty.body: Dulce is not a medical device. It will not tell you how much insulin to take. That's what your endo is for — Dulce only helps you understand better.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Have a question, idea or feedback? Get in touch.
contact.headline: We're here
contact.subheadline: For beta questions, press, partnerships or anything else, write to us directly.

beta.title: Can I be a beta tester?
beta.body: Fill out the form on the <link>beta page</link> and we'll get back to you.

press.title: Press and interviews
press.body: Happy to talk. Write to hola@dulceglucosa.com with "Press" in the subject.

doctor.title: I'm an endocrinologist — can we collaborate?
doctor.body: Yes. We're looking for clinicians who'd like to try Dulce with their patients in the beta. Get in touch.

waitlist.headline: Join the waitlist
waitlist.subheadline: We'll let you know when Dulce launches. No spam, no sharing of your email.
waitlist.consent: I agree to be contacted by Dulce about the launch. More info in the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.
waitlist.success: Done. We'll let you know when it's ready.

follow.headline: You've been invited to follow someone on Dulce
follow.body: Dulce lets a family member share their glucose readings with you in real time. Install the app, then enter this code to start following.
follow.getApp: Get Dulce on the App Store

steps.title: How it works
steps.one: Install Dulce from the App Store.
steps.two: Open the app and tap “Follow someone”.
steps.three: Enter the code above to connect.

follow.expiryNote: Invite codes expire 24 hours after they're created. Ask for a fresh one if this stops working.
follow.noCode: No invite code found in this link. Ask for a new invite.
